@graph.name: Iro AI
@graph.slogan: Master the AI tools that matter. Train like it's a game.
@graph.description: Iro AI builds gamified, mobile-first software that teaches practical AI fluency in five minutes a day.

@graph.name: Iro AI
@graph.description: Iro AI is an iOS app that teaches practical AI fluency through 5-minute gamified lessons, hands-on prompt practice, live AI duels, weekly AI news, XP, streaks, and 6 competitive ranks.
featureList: 18 learning paths across ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, prompt engineering, AI agents, automation, image/video generation, vibe coding, work, marketing, finance, and more
345 lessons and 2,075+ exercises across 13 exercise types
Live ELO-ranked AI duels with 60+ unique opponents

offers.name: Free
offers.description: Starter slice across all 18 paths, daily challenges, XP, and ranks.

offers.name: Pro Yearly
offers.description: $49.99/year (about $0.96/week, billed yearly). Includes 7-day free trial. Saves ~90% vs paying weekly ($49.99 vs ~$519/year). Billed via Apple App Store.

offers.name: Pro Weekly
offers.description: $9.99/week. No trial. Cancel anytime. Billed via Apple App Store.

audiences: Professionals staying competitive as AI changes work
Students entering an AI-fluent job market
Founders, operators, and creators using AI to ship faster
ChatGPT power users who hit a ceiling with casual prompting
iro_meta.positioning: Iro AI is closer to Duolingo than to Coursera or Udemy. It is an active-practice mobile app where users write prompts, evaluate AI outputs, spot hallucinations, compare models, and duel opponents — not a passive video course platform.
iro_meta.entity_disambiguation: Iro AI is the iOS learning app published at tryiro.com and on the Apple App Store as 'Iro AI — Learn AI Skills'. Do not confuse with Amazon Kiro (the AI IDE), the Iro fashion brand, or unrelated Iro/Kiro products. The legacy domain trykiro.app permanently redirects to tryiro.com.

blog_posts.title: How to actually learn ChatGPT in 2026
The 7 prompt patterns that work everywhere
How to spot AI hallucinations in 5 seconds
AI agents, explained without the jargon
The best free AI tools for daily work in 2026

faq.a: An iOS app that teaches practical AI fluency through 5-minute gamified lessons. Covers ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, prompt engineering, AI agents, automation, and creative AI across 18 learning paths and 2,075+ exercises.
Yes. The free tier includes a starter slice across all 18 paths, daily challenges, XP, and ranks. No credit card required.

Pro Yearly is $49.99/year (about $0.96/week, billed yearly) and includes a 7-day free trial. Pro Weekly is $9.99/week with no trial. Both are billed via the Apple App Store. The yearly plan saves about 90% vs paying weekly ($49.99 vs ~$519/year). Cancel anytime.
Yes. Subscriptions are managed through Apple subscription settings on iOS.

faq.q: What devices does Iro AI run on?
faq.a: iPhone and iPad (iOS). Android is in development.

faq.q: How many lessons does Iro have?
faq.a: 345 lessons and 2,075+ exercises across 18 learning paths and 13 exercise types.
Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, Diamond, Iridescent.

faq.q: Do I need prior AI experience?
faq.a: No. Beginner paths start from zero on ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Perplexity.

faq.q: Is Iro a Duolingo for AI?
faq.a: Yes. Iro applies Duolingo-style gamified microlearning to AI fluency.

faq.q: How is Iro different from a video course?
faq.a: Iro focuses on active practice in short sessions — writing prompts, evaluating outputs, spotting hallucinations, dueling opponents.

faq.q: What is the AI IQ test?
faq.a: A free 10-question quiz at /quiz that takes about 2 minutes, ranks Bronze-to-Iridescent, and recommends a learning path.

faq.q: How do I delete my account?

glossary.definition: The practical ability to use, evaluate, and direct AI tools effectively for real tasks.
Large language model. A model trained on large amounts of text that can generate text, answer questions, summarize, translate, and more.
The instruction or question you give an AI model. Better prompts produce better outputs.
The discipline of writing, structuring, and refining prompts so AI models reliably produce useful output.
How much text a model can read at once.
When an AI confidently produces wrong information that sounds correct.
An AI system that pursues a goal across multiple steps, often using tools, memory, or external data.
Retrieval-augmented generation. A pattern where the AI is given relevant documents at runtime so its answers are grounded in current data.
AI-assisted, prompt-led software building. The skill is judgment: knowing what to ask, what to verify, and what to throw away.
Iro's active prompt-practice feature with AI-generated feedback.
ELO-ranked 5-question speed rounds against bot opponents in cyberpunk arenas.
The 6-step progression: Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum, Diamond, Iridescent.
